title: Test-Traceability Manifest v3
description: Generated compliance artifact (.shipwright/compliance/test-traceability.json) joining requirements to their tagged tests per layer, with per-test execution evidence. Frozen contract for the requirement->test traceability campaign (Spec test-traceability-across-layers.md sec 11). The committed artifact is derived/RTM-visibility only; enforcing gates regenerate base+head themselves (R3). v3: the requirement key namespace derives from the FR id's group digits (01::FR-01.03) instead of the spec's parent directory name (01-adopted::FR-01.03), so a directory rename no longer rewrites every key. Requirement INNER fields are unchanged from v2.

schema_version.description: Pinned manifest schema version. Travels with requirement_model.MODEL_VERSION.

collector_version.description: Version of the collector that produced this artifact (audit provenance, R3).

source_commit.description: The head commit the manifest was generated from (audit provenance, R3).

spec_hash.description: Hash of the spec inputs, so a stale/hand-edited manifest is detectable (R3).

requirements.description: Requirements keyed by namespace::FR-XX.YY, where the namespace is the FR id's group digits (v3: 01::FR-01.03). The pattern pins the namespace to exactly two digits, so an obviously path-derived namespace (01-adopted::FR-01.03) fails validation at write time. Shape alone cannot express that the two halves AGREE (01::FR-02.03 matches it) -- collectors._test_links_requirements.assert_keys_derive_from_ids carries that half.

invalid_layers.description: FRs whose explicitly-headed Layers cell resolved to zero valid canonical layers (author typo/synonym). Optional + additive (older manifests omit it); kept `explicit` upstream so the D-layer hard gate still fires, surfaced here so the finding can name the raw token (Spec §11-R4).

untagged_tests.description: Tests with no @FR tag (informational, not a hard gate).

invalid_ids.description: Rows the FR-table reader declined to treat as requirements, with the reason: `non_canonical_id` (FR-shaped but not the canonical FR-XX.YY form -- e.g. the uncapped `FR-01.100` an adopt run emits past 99 detected routes), `no_governing_header`, or `row_narrower_than_header`. Optional + additive, and OMITTED ENTIRELY when nothing was declined, so a clean repo emits a byte-identical manifest (same churn rule as `fold_map`). Informational -- the reader never raises; this exists so a dropped row is reported rather than silent.

fold_map.description: The merged '## FR-Fold-Map' alias table in force: folded FR id -> its surviving capability FR. Optional + additive, and OMITTED ENTIRELY when the repo declares no fold-map, so a project without one emits a byte-identical manifest. A tag on a folded id is filed against the survivor (see testLink.resolved_from) instead of orphaning.

fold_defects.description: Fold-map hygiene problems (cycle, conflicting/dangling/removed survivor, self-fold, unparsable row, folded id still active). Each fails CLOSED upstream — the affected tag keeps its orphan status — and is surfaced here so a broken alias table is loud rather than silently under-resolving. Omitted when empty.

coverageOkImpliesPassing.$comment: R1 skipped != covered, enforced structurally: coverage.<layer> == 'ok' REQUIRES tests.<layer> to contain a test link that is BOTH status 'enabled' AND executed 'pass'. A skipped/not_run/failed link can never yield 'ok'.

requiredLayersHaveCoverage.$comment: Every required layer must have a coverage entry — a required layer cannot be silently omitted from coverage (its absence would hide an uncovered layer).

allOf.$comment: A removed FR carries no live tests (its tests become orphans). removed => tests is empty.

spec_path.description: Relative path to the spec.md the FR lives in.

required_layers_source.description: Provenance of required_layers. D-layer WARNs on legacy-missing, FAILs on post-rollout omission (R4).

tests.description: Tagged tests bound to this FR, grouped by layer. A layer key may be omitted or empty. Each link's own 'layer' field MUST match the group it is filed under (so unit coverage cannot be claimed from an e2e test link).

coverage.description: Coverage-at-layer status. 'ok' REQUIRES a tagged test that is enabled AND executed=pass (R1); a skipped/not_run test is 'MISSING'.

id.description: Stable test id, keyed to execution evidence.

status.description: Static disposition of the test in source.

executed.description: Observed result from this run's execution evidence (R1). Only 'pass' + status 'enabled' yields coverage 'ok'.

tag_source.description: Which grammar form bound this tag (fr_tag_grammar.TAG_SOURCES).

resolved_from.description: PROVENANCE ONLY. Present when the source tag named a FOLDED id and was resolved through fold_map to the surviving FR this link is filed under. The requirement this link covers is always the survivor (the node the link sits in); resolved_from records the id the test source literally carries, so an auditor reading @covers("FR-01.44") can see why it counts toward FR-01.28. Omitted for a direct (non-folded) tag.

kind.description: Closed vocabulary (fr_fold_map.FOLD_DEFECT_KINDS), pinned by a drift-guard test.

folded.description: The folded id the defect is about (or '?' for an unparsable row). For a cycle, the smallest member id, so one loop yields one stable entry.

survivor.description: The survivor id the edge pointed at, when parseable.

spec_path.description: Relative path to the spec.md the fold-map row lives in.

line.description: 1-based source line of the offending row, so the defect is actionable.

raw.description: Bounded, whitespace-flattened source row (<=160 chars). Repo-controlled text — a renderer MUST escape it.

allOf.$comment: A confirmed_orphan has an explicit tag pointing at a dead/removed FR, so its tagged_fr must be a real FR id. An unmapped orphan has no FR match, so tagged_fr is null.

oneOf.description: null when category is 'unmapped' — the test maps to no live FR (R4).

reason.description: fr_removed: FR is in ## Removed Requirements. fr_absent: FR is not in the live spec at all.

category.description: confirmed_orphan: explicit tag -> dead/removed FR. possible_orphan: heuristic/ambiguous. unmapped: no match (R4).

test.description: Test the malformed tag was found on (may be empty if unbound).

reason.description: Optional diagnostic for why the tag is invalid, e.g. 'non_canonical_fr_id' | 'non_string_arg' (fr_tag_grammar.InvalidTag.reason). Optional so a bare {test, raw} entry still validates.

fr.description: The declined row's first cell, verbatim. Deliberately NOT pattern-constrained to the canonical form -- the whole point is that this value is not canonical, so pinning it to FR-XX.YY would make the diagnostic unrepresentable.

spec_path.description: Relative path to the spec.md the row lives in.

raw.description: The declined row's cells, joined, truncated to 200 chars.

reason.description: Why the reader declined the row. A closed vocabulary so a new reject class cannot arrive unannounced.

fr.description: The FR whose Layers cell is unrecognized.

spec_path.description: Relative path to the spec.md the FR lives in.

raw.description: The unrecognized Layers cell text, e.g. 'int, db'.
